product.name: Proof of Possible
product.thesis: A private, local-first browser app that turns an overwhelming goal into one small falsifiable real-world experiment and records evidence, replacing paralysis with evidence-backed agency.
product.ethicalBoundary: The product does not trivialize structural barriers. A blocked result is valid evidence.

strategyLenses.sourceSummary: JTBD Proof of Possible—structured brief, risk assumption, 48-hour evidence experiment
strategyLenses.proposalSummary: Frame the job as producing credible evidence instead of completing the full ambition.
strategyLenses.rationale: Strong thesis and outcome fit; the 48-hour horizon was compressed to a ten-minute first-use loop.

strategyLenses.sourceSummary: constraint-first private goal→constraint→experiment with local continuity and production spine
strategyLenses.proposalSummary: Start from the binding constraint and preserve a private, resumable experiment state.
strategyLenses.rationale: Highest alignment with honesty, privacy, feasibility, and an end-to-end Frame 1 production spine.

strategyLenses.sourceSummary: growth Proof Card with useful forks
strategyLenses.proposalSummary: Create a compact proof artifact that could later support useful, non-performative sharing and forks.
strategyLenses.rationale: The receipt is valuable, but public sharing was deferred because privacy and core-loop evidence come first.

strategyLenses.sourceSummary: behavior-change Impossible/10 with immutable micro-deliverable, sprint, behavior receipt
strategyLenses.proposalSummary: Use a hard ten-minute ceiling, preserve the original promise, and close with a behavior receipt.
strategyLenses.rationale: Directly attacks avoidance while immutable provenance prevents quiet goalpost movement.

strategyLenses.sourceSummary: emotional Proof of Possible with Name/Proof Turn/Evidence Receipt
strategyLenses.proposalSummary: Move the user through naming, a single proof turn, and a dignified evidence receipt.
strategyLenses.rationale: Gives the product a humane emotional arc without relying on cheerleading or false certainty.

strategyLenses.sourceSummary: technical local-first browser tool with core journey/persistence/CI
strategyLenses.proposalSummary: Ship a zero-dependency static browser app with pure domain logic, local persistence, tests, and CI.
strategyLenses.rationale: Best fit for GitHub Pages, privacy, resilience, auditability, and zero-friction maintenance.

strategyLenses.sourceSummary: trust/accessibility impossibility debugger with inspectable hypothesis and private timeline
strategyLenses.proposalSummary: Make the hypothesis inspectable, controls accessible, constraints explicit, and history private.
strategyLenses.rationale: Trust is essential for sensitive goals; inspectability and accessibility are product behavior, not polish.

strategyLenses.sourceSummary: differentiated renter evidence packet (minority vertical-market proposal)
strategyLenses.proposalSummary: Specialize the evidence flow into a renter-oriented packet for a concrete high-stakes use case.
strategyLenses.rationale: A useful minority direction, but too narrow for validating the general core loop in Frame 1.

consensus.scoringMethod: Eight strategy proposals were scored on thesis fit, user value, trust, evidence quality, and Frame 1 feasibility; each dimension contributed up to five points.
consensus.decisionRationale: The convergence favored a private constraint-first loop: name the goal and barrier, create one inspectable experiment, run a real wall-clock sprint, and preserve any result as evidence. Growth sharing and the renter vertical were deliberately deferred.
convergedPrinciples: Agency comes from evidence, not motivation theater.
The smallest test must still touch reality and be falsifiable.
Structural barriers remain explicit; blocked is a first-class outcome.
Private local continuity precedes social or growth mechanics.
The original mission and every simplification remain inspectable.

selectedMutations.name: NAME + COMPRESS THE IMPOSSIBLE
selectedMutations.summary: Accessible intake captures goal, meaning, binding obstacle, observable proof, a one-to-ten-minute timebox, and baseline confidence, then deterministically produces one editable experiment.

selectedMutations.name: RUN ONE PROOF SPRINT
selectedMutations.summary: A reload-persistent wall-clock countdown has no pause and closes only through completed, attempted, or blocked; simplification preserves provenance and the original deadline.

selectedMutations.name: EVIDENCE RECEIPT + VALUE MEASUREMENT
selectedMutations.summary: A local receipt records evidence and post-confidence, displays confidence delta and history, and measures First-Evidence Rate.

name-compress: All six intake dimensions are labeled, keyboard reachable, and validated.
Timebox is a whole number from one through ten minutes.
Generation is deterministic and the mission, observable success, and stop condition remain editable.
Empty or invalid intake and edited plans cannot advance.
proof-sprint: The timer is based on a persisted wall-clock deadline and survives reload.
There is no pause control or hidden deadline reset.
The only recorded outcomes are completed, attempted, and blocked.
A user can simplify a running mission while retaining original plan, revisions, reason, and deadline.
evidence-receipt: An outcome requires an evidence note or a valid HTTP(S) URL.
The receipt displays baseline confidence, post-confidence, and signed delta.
Goals and receipts persist in local browser history without telemetry.
First-Evidence Rate equals goals with outcomes divided by goals created and is visible.
The UI consumes a static 12-frame evolution timeline with Frame 1 shipped and later frames pending.
production: Responsive semantic UI includes visible focus, clear errors, keyboard controls, and reduced-motion handling.
No third-party runtime requests, backend, account, or AI dependency exists.
Core state, validation, timer, metrics, serialization, and deployment invariants have automated tests.
GitHub Actions validates main and deploys the static site through GitHub Pages.

live.source: Computed locally from persisted goal and outcome records
live.formula: goals with recorded outcomes / goals created
